title: 7 Powerful Herbal Essential Oils for Frozen Shoulder Recovery

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is not present, it's actually Curcumin is found in turmeric, Curcumin in Salvia divinorum is not proven to be present, but Curcumin is not accurate, however, Curcumin found in turmeric is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the shoulder joint.
Demethoxycurcumin is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the affected area.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the shoulder joint, allowing for increased mobility and flexibility.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oil, also known as turmeric oil, is a natural remedy that can help alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder. This condition causes stiffness and pain in the shoulder joint, making everyday activities difficult. Turmeric oil contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, it can reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area. The oil also has antioxidant properties, which can help repair damaged tissues and promote healing in the shoulder joint. Additionally, turmeric oil has a warming effect on the muscles, which can increase blood flow and help to relax tense muscles. This can help to improve range of motion and reduce stiffness in the shoulder, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil for frozen shoulder, you'll need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass container. First, dry the Salvia officinalis leaves thoroughly in a warm, dark place for a few days, or use a food dehydrator. Next, fill a clean glass container with the dried leaves, making sure they're completely covered by the carrier oil. Seal the container and let it steep in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the container occasionally. After the steeping time, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Finally, store the Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder, by mixing a few drops with a carrier oil and applying it topically to the affected area.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for frozen shoulder

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as ginger oil, is a natural remedy that can help with frozen shoulder. Frozen shoulder, also known as adhesive capsulitis, is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes inflamed and painful, making it hard to move the arm. Ginger oil contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the shoulder joint. When applied topically, ginger oil can relax the muscles and improve blood flow, helping to reduce stiffness and promote healing. Additionally, ginger oil has analgesic properties that can help numb the pain, making it easier to move the arm. By reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and numbing pain, ginger oil can provide relief for people suffering from frozen shoulder.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil that helps with frozen shoulder, you will need dried Salvia leaves, a carrier oil, and a clean glass jar. First, fill a clean glass jar with about 2-3 inches of dried Salvia leaves. Then, pour a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered. Next, place the jar in a sunny spot and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called infusion, and it helps to release the beneficial properties of the Salvia leaves into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. You can then store the Salvia oil in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to help soothe and reduce inflammation in frozen shoulder. To use, apply a few drops of the oil to the affected area, massage it in gently, and enjoy the benefits of this natural remedy.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for frozen shoulder

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in frozen shoulder. The oil's active compound, eucalyptol, acts as a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relax the muscles and joints. This can make it easier to move the shoulder and improve flexibility. Additionally, the oil's decongestant properties can help reduce fluid buildup in the shoulder joint, further relieving pain and stiffness. Eucalyptus globulus oil can also help to improve blood circulation, which is essential for healing and reducing inflammation. When applied topically, the oil can be absorbed into the skin, allowing its active compounds to target the affected area and provide relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils that help with frozen shoulder, you will need fresh Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar. First, pick a bunch of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and wash them in cold water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, dry the leaves thoroughly with a clean towel to prevent any bacterial growth. In a clean glass jar, combine the dried Salvia officinalis leaves with a carrier oil, making sure the leaves are completely submerged in the oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil extract from the leaves. After the extraction period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil is now ready to use. To help with frozen shoulder, gently massage a few drops of the oil onto the affected area, or add it to a warm bath for relaxation and pain relief.  Note: Please consult a healthcare professional before using herbal essential oils, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

Some people also use it to improve circulation, which can help promote healing and reduce stiffness. remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum essential oil comes from the cayenne pepper plant, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the body. Frozen shoulder is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful, making it hard to move. The heat from Capsicum annuum essential oil can increase blood flow to the affected area, which may help loosen up the stiff joint and reduce pain. It can also reduce the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body. Capsicum annuum essential oil can be applied topically, using a carrier oil, to the affected area, which can help reduce pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Some people also use it in a warm bath or as a massage oil to help relax the muscles and increase blood flow to the shoulder area.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil that can help with frozen shoulder, you will need fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. First, dry the fresh leaves by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the leaves are completely dry, chop them finely and place them in the glass jar. Next, pour the carrier oil over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil extract from the leaves. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This Salvia officinalis oil can be used topically to help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder, but be sure to dilute it with a carrier oil before applying to the skin and perform a patch test first to check for any sensitivity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil, also known as licorice root oil, is a natural remedy that may help alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. This condition causes limited mobility and pain in the shoulder joint due to inflammation and scar tissue buildup. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root oil may help reduce swelling and ease pain by blocking the production of chemical messengers that cause inflammation. Additionally, licorice root oil has antioxidant properties that help protect the joint from further damage and promote healing. Its anti-arthritic properties may also help reduce the production of compounds that cause joint pain and stiffness, making it easier to move the shoulder joint and regain mobility. Some studies suggest that applying licorice root oil topically may also help reduce oxidative stress and promote collagen synthesis, which can aid in the repair of damaged tissues and improve joint function.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil that may help with frozen shoulder, you'll first need to harvest the Salvia officinalis leaves, also known as sage leaves. Choose fresh leaves with no signs of damage or disease. Next, dry the leaves thoroughly in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator. Once the leaves are completely dry, you can create a tea by steeping 1 part of the dried leaves in 2 parts of boiling water for 10-15 minutes. Let it cool, then strain the liquid and discard the solids. Now, you can make an infusion by mixing the sage tea with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:5 ratio, and letting it sit for 2-3 weeks in a dark glass bottle, shaking it every day. After the infusion is complete, use a coffee filter or cheesecloth to strain the liquid and discard the solids. The resulting essential oil can be used topically, but make sure to dilute it with a carrier oil first, and always do a patch test before using it on the affected area.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for frozen shoulder

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage essential oil, can help relieve frozen shoulder pain. This is because sage oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the affected area. When the shoulder joint swells, it becomes stiff and painful, making it hard to move. Sage oil's anti-inflammatory properties calm down the swelling, allowing the joint to move freely again. Additionally, sage oil has analgesic properties, which ease the pain in the shoulder. When applied topically, sage oil can also increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting faster healing. This helps repair damaged tissues and restore normal function to the shoulder joint, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils that help with frozen shoulder, you'll need fresh or dried sage leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. First, prepare the sage leaves by rinsing them with clean water and patting them dry with a paper towel. Then, in a clean glass jar, combine 1 cup of sage leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil, making sure the leaves are completely covered. Next, store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil extract from the sage leaves. After the extraction period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the sage leaves. The resulting herbal essential oil can be used topically by applying a few drops to the affected area 2-3 times a day, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing in frozen shoulder.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for frozen shoulder

intro_desc: Frozen shoulder, also known as adhesive capsulitis, is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful, limiting movement and daily activities. To alleviate this condition, herbal essential oils like Curcuma longa and Zingiber officinale can be used due to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, contains a compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and pain in the shoulder joint. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has a compound called gingerol, which has natural pain-relieving properties that can help to reduce pain and stiffness in the affected area.  When the shoulder is frozen, simple tasks like getting dressed, cooking, or even sleeping can become challenging. This can lead to feelings of frustration, isolation, and decreased quality of life. Fixing the issue is crucial because it can improve mobility, reduce pain, and restore daily activities. Using herbal essential oils like Curcuma longa and Zingiber officinale can be a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms and promote healing. To use these oils, they can be applied topically to the affected area, usually mixed with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any new treatment, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.  Using herbal essential oils like Curcuma longa and Zingiber officinale can be a beneficial addition to traditional treatment methods, such as physical therapy or medication. By incorporating these oils into your treatment plan, you can potentially reduce pain, improve mobility, and regain independence, leading to a significant improvement in your quality of life.
supplementary_best: A combination of 'Curcuma longa', 'Zingiber officinale', and 'Salvia officinalis' essential oils may help remedy frozen shoulder. 'Curcuma longa' oil, also known as turmeric oil, contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder. 'Zingiber officinale' oil, or ginger oil, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relax muscles and reduce stiffness. 'Salvia officinalis' oil, or sage oil, contains antispasmodic properties that can help relax muscles and improve blood flow to the affected area. This combination of oils may work together to provide relief from frozen shoulder symptoms by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving blood flow.
supplementary_avoid: If you have frozen shoulder, there are some herbal essential oils you should avoid. One of them is Wintergreen (Gaultheria procumbens). This oil has a compound called methyl salicylate, which can increase blood flow and reduce pain. However, it can also thin the blood and increase the risk of bleeding. This could be a problem if you have frozen shoulder and are taking blood-thinning medication or have a bleeding disorder.  Another oil to avoid is Birch (Betula alba). Like Wintergreen, it also contains methyl salicylate, which can increase the risk of bleeding.  You should also avoid Clary Sage (Salvia sclarea) if you have frozen shoulder. This oil can slow down the movement of your muscles and joints, which can make it harder to move your arm and shoulder.  Lastly, avoid Pennyroyal (Mentha pulegium) if you have frozen shoulder. This oil can cause liver damage and interact with certain medications, including those you might be taking for your frozen shoulder.
supplementary_other_remedies: Heat from warm compresses can help relax the muscles around the shoulder, reducing pain and stiffness. Soaking in a warm bath with Epsom salt can also bring relief by reducing inflammation. Cold packs or an ice pack wrapped in a towel can be applied to the shoulder for short periods to numb the pain. Resting the shoulder and avoiding heavy lifting can also help prevent further injury. Gentle stretching and exercises can be done to maintain flexibility and strength, but it's essential to avoid pushing the shoulder past its comfort zone. Applying pressure to specific points on the body, like the base of the neck, can also help relieve tension and promote healing. Additionally, some people find relief from using a foam roller or a massage ball to roll out the tight muscles in the shoulder area.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with frozen shoulder may also experience pain and stiffness in their neck, upper back, and scapula (shoulder blade). This is often due to overuse or poor posture. Medicinal herbs like turmeric, ginger, and willow bark can help alleviate this pain. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain. Ginger has a similar effect, and its anti-inflammatory compounds can also help relax tense muscles. Willow bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that can help reduce pain and inflammation.  Additionally, people with frozen shoulder may experience numbness or tingling in their arm or hand, which can be caused by compressed nerves. St. John's Wort and cayenne pepper can help with this. St. John's Wort contains a compound called hyperforin, which can help reduce inflammation and ease pain. Cayenne pepper contains capsaicin, which can help relax tense muscles and increase blood flow to the affected area.  Some people with frozen shoulder may also experience fatigue and weakness in their arm or shoulder, which can be caused by poor circulation and inflammation. Ginkgo biloba and ashwagandha can help with this. Ginkgo biloba can help improve circulation and reduce inflammation, while ashwagandha can help reduce stress and promote relaxation.  Finally, people with frozen shoulder may experience difficulty sleeping due to pain and discomfort. Valerian root and passionflower can help with this. Valerian root can help promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, while passionflower can help reduce pain and promote a good night's sleep.
